prompt: A SentinelFit Operations platform capable of managing offline member access, coaching content performance, and full-lifecycle face data governance for a gym or training studio environment. The Vue.js web UI (English) starts with a local account sign-in that supports quick re-entry on the same workstation (optional 4–6 digit PIN after a successful password login) and optional phone number binding strictly as a contact attribute with no SMS or verification; roles are Member (user), Coach, and Administrator with a clear hierarchy and role-based navigation. Members can enroll with a coach, consent to face enrollment, and complete face capture at a front desk station using a connected camera or image import, receiving immediate on-screen feedback for quality gates and duplication warnings. Coaches publish training tips and studio announcements, then review in-app analytics such as content views by source channel (e.g., lobby kiosk, front-desk terminal), top-performing posts, and onsite search term trends; they can filter by date range (MM/DD/YYYY) and location, and drill into linked charts without leaving the page. Administrators use a configurable dashboard builder with drag-and-drop charts and reusable report templates, exportable to CSV/Excel/PDF, and can set scheduled generation (for example, every Monday at 6:00 AM) with “subscriptions” delivered as an in-app report inbox and a downloadable file stored on a designated shared folder path, keeping the workflow fully offline. The backend uses Express (TypeScript) to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend, enforcing server-side validation, session management, and auditability, with MySQL as the system of record for users, roles, content events, face records, and operational logs. Passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts; logout immediately revokes the session. Sensitive fields (e.g., face template identifiers, contact phone, and any government ID fields if configured) are encrypted at rest using AES-256 with per-environment keys stored locally and rotated every 180 days, while UI views mask values by default (e.g., showing only last 4 digits of a phone number) under least-privilege access. API access control includes IP allowlisting, rate limiting (60 requests per minute per IP), and request signing via HMAC-SHA256 with a nonce and timestamp window of ±5 minutes to prevent replay. Face lifecycle management includes import/capture constraints (JPG/PNG only, max 5 MB, minimum 640×480), basic quality checks (blur score threshold, face-in-frame requirement, and a simple two-step liveness challenge requiring two timed captures with a prompted head turn), deduplication using a local similarity threshold (configurable, default 0.85), versioning on updates, deactivation instead of hard delete, and immutable audit trail retention for 7 years. Observability runs entirely on-prem: application logs, performance metrics, and anomaly alerts surface on an admin console (no external services), with access logs retained 180 days; nightly encrypted backups run at 2:00 AM, retain 30 daily restore points, and support tested restore workflows to a standby MySQL instance.
